data.title: As a plugin maintainer, new commits are blocked from re-introducing plugins/dso self-references
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Create .claude/hooks/pre-commit/check-plugin-self-ref.sh — a project-local pre-commit hook that scans staged files under plugins/dso/ for the string plugins/dso and blocks the commit unless the match is on a line annotated with `# plugin-self-ref-ok: <reason>`. Add a test in tests/hooks/. Verify grep -r "plugins/dso" plugins/dso/ returns only suppressed lines.
**Why**: Without enforcement, the zero-occurrence state achieved by S1-S3 can regress on any commit.
**Scope**:
- IN: .claude/hooks/pre-commit/check-plugin-self-ref.sh; tests/hooks/test-plugin-self-ref.sh; final zero-occurrence verification
- OUT: The hook itself may contain plugins/dso in its grep pattern (it lives in .claude/hooks/, not plugins/dso/)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a commit staging a file under plugins/dso/ containing plugins/dso (without a `# plugin-self-ref-ok:` annotation on that line) is blocked by the hook with an actionable error message
  ← Satisfies: "pre-commit hook blocks commits that introduce the string plugins/dso into any file under plugins/dso/"
- When this story is complete, a line annotated with `# plugin-self-ref-ok: <reason>` passes the hook check without blocking
  ← Satisfies: "pre-commit hook blocks commits (with suppression support for legitimate git-relative patterns)"
- When this story is complete, `grep -rn "plugins/dso" plugins/dso/` returns only lines annotated with `# plugin-self-ref-ok:` (zero unannotated matches)
  ← Satisfies: "grep -r 'plugins/dso' plugins/dso/ returns zero matches"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Test both blocking (unannotated match) and pass-through (annotated plugin-self-ref-ok) cases
- [Reliability] Hook must work in both local dev and CI contexts

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
